result:
I would dismantle the illusion of separateness. 

If I could alter one fundamental mechanism of the world, I would wire humanity with perfect, involuntary empathy—the absolute inability to interact with another living being without instantly and vividly experiencing their emotional and physical state as if it were your own.

Right now, almost all systemic and interpersonal suffering thrives in the dark, silent gap between minds. Cruelty, exploitation, hoarding, and violence are only possible because of abstraction. It is terrifyingly easy to harm someone, or to look away from their misery, as long as they remain an "other." Their pain is muffled by the impenetrable walls of the individual skull. 

If those walls vanished—if striking someone meant feeling the exact sting of the blow on your own skin, if turning away a refugee meant suffocating under their exact terror, if hoarding wealth meant intimately feeling the gnawing hunger of the starving—the entire architecture of human civilization would rewrite itself overnight. 

War would become a psychological impossibility. Systemic poverty would be treated as an agonizing global emergency rather than a political talking point. Deception would become useless. 

It wouldn't be a magic trick that generates infinite clean energy or cures disease, but it wouldn't need to be. By forcing humanity to actually experience the reality of its own interconnectedness, the species would finally be compelled to solve those problems together, simply out of a newly shared instinct for self-preservation.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
